title: codecrafters-io-build-your-own-x
summary: This repo understands that the deepest path to mastering a technology is building it from scratch.  It curates the 'learn by building' pedagogy across 35+ technology domains, organized around Feynman's principle.
creator_view: This repo understands that the deepest path to mastering a technology is building it from scratch. It curates the 'learn by building' pedagogy across 35+ technology domains, organized around Feynman's principle. The taxonomy — from 3D renderers to web servers — maps the landscape of implementable systems knowledge.

candidates.name: Build Your Own React (Pomber)
candidates.description: https://pomb.us/build-your-own-react/. Canonical React-from-scratch tutorial. Fiber, reconciliation, hooks. Directly applicable — React 19 in SoNash stack.

candidates.name: Didact: DIY React
candidates.description: https://github.com/hexacta/didact. Virtual DOM diffing focus. Complementary to Pomber. Understanding re-render behavior.

candidates.name: Write Yourself a Git
candidates.description: https://wyag.thb.lt. Python git-from-scratch. Understanding object model helps design better hook/worktree infrastructure.

candidates.name: Regex Matching Can Be Simple and Fast (Russ Cox)
candidates.description: https://swtch.com/~rsc/regexp/regexp1.html. NFA/DFA theory. Directly applicable to check-pattern-compliance.js regex performance.

candidates.name: 31-Category Tutorial Taxonomy
candidates.description: 390 links across 31 domains. 16 individually evaluated in content-eval.jsonl. Cross-reference against 72-skill index.

candidates.name: Curated Link Format Pattern
candidates.description: Standard awesome-list format. Low extraction value.

candidates.name: Skill Retirement Process Design
candidates.description: Case study: 486K stars, 462 open issues, 1 commit/90d. What happens when a curated collection grows without retirement/archival. Directly applicable to managing 72+ skills in JASON-OS.

candidates.name: Build from Scratch Framing for Domain 01
candidates.description: Feynman principle applied to system understanding. Structure JASON-OS Internal Archaeology (Domain 01) as 'how each system was built' narratives rather than 'what exists' inventories.

candidates.name: Celebrity Stagnation Lifecycle
candidates.description: Community enthusiasm → commercial acquisition → maintenance bottleneck → dormancy. Cross-ref: overlaps with public-apis stagnation entry.

candidates.name: Shell/CLI Tutorial Collection
candidates.description: 7 shell tutorials (C, Go, Rust). Canonical references if JASON-OS needs custom command dispatch. Defer until architecture solidifies.

candidates.name: Single-File-Everything Anti-Pattern
candidates.description: 390 links in one README.md. Watch for same impulse in EXTRACTIONS.md, MEMORY.md, SKILL_INDEX.md — when a single file becomes the dumping ground.

candidates.name: Inline License Without LICENSE File
candidates.description: CC0 in README but no LICENSE file. GitHub API can't detect it. Always include machine-readable LICENSE if publishing.

maintainability.note: Markdown only, sparse commits, bottleneck maintainer

process.note: No license, no releases, commercial ownership

absencePattern.description: Massive stars + minimal recent activity + commercial capture by codecrafters-io. 462 open issues with 1 commit in 90 days indicates bottlenecked maintenance.

creatorLens: This repo understands that the deepest path to mastering a technology is building it from scratch. It curates the 'learn by building' pedagogy across 35+ technology domains, organized around Feynman's principle. The taxonomy — from 3D renderers to web servers — maps the landscape of implementable systems knowledge.
